GARDEN PLACE HEALTHCARE

CCN: 225267 · ATTLEBORO, MA 02703 · Bristol County

Overview

Address
193-195 PLEASANT STREET, ATTLEBORO, MA 02703
Phone
5082224950
Certified beds
133
Avg daily residents
114 (86% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit LLC
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1981-06-01
Setting
Urban
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.35 4.17 3.10 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.64 0.73 0.60 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.80 0.92 0.74 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.90 2.51 1.76 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.45 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.05 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.08
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.58
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
2.86
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
25.7%
annual
RN turnover
42.9%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.48
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.07
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-11-06 10 10 0 48 1 48
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-08-16 24 22 2 196 2 294
Total weighted health score
109.5
lower is better

Deficiencies (46)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

44 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
2 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0551 Give the resident's representative the ability to exercise the resident's rights. D 2024-11-06 2024-12-05
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-11-06 2024-12-05
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2024-11-06 2024-12-05
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2024-11-06 2024-12-05
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. D 2024-11-06 2024-12-05
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2024-11-06 2024-12-05
0699 Provide care or services that was trauma informed and/or culturally competent. D 2024-11-06 2024-12-05
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2024-11-06 2024-12-05
0791 Provide or obtain dental services for each resident. D 2024-11-06 2024-12-05
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2024-11-06 2024-12-05
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. G 2024-11-06 2024-12-05
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2024-10-01 2024-10-17
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2023-08-16 2023-09-13
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2023-08-16 2023-09-13
0585 Honor the resident's right to voice grievances without discrimination or reprisal and the facility must establish a grievance policy and make prompt efforts to resolve grievances. E 2023-08-16 2023-09-13
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. D 2023-08-16 2023-09-13
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2023-08-16 2023-09-13
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2023-08-16 2023-09-13
0645 PASARR screening for Mental disorders or Intellectual Disabilities E 2023-08-16 2023-09-13
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2023-08-16 2023-09-13
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2023-08-16 2023-09-13
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. G 2023-08-16 2023-09-13
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. E 2023-08-16 2023-09-13
0700 Try different approaches before using a bed rail. If a bed rail is needed, the facility must (1) assess a resident for safety risk; (2) review these risks and benefits with the resident/representative; (3) get informed consent; and (4) Correctly install and maintain the bed rail. E 2023-08-16 2023-09-13
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2023-08-16 2023-09-13
0770 Provide timely, quality laboratory services/tests to meet the needs of residents. D 2023-08-16 2023-09-13
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. E 2023-08-16 2023-09-13
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2023-08-16 2023-09-13
0813 Have a policy regarding use and storage of foods brought to residents by family and other visitors. D 2023-08-16 2023-09-13
0865 Have a plan that describes the process for conducting QAPI and QAA activities. F 2023-08-16 2023-09-13
0867 Set up an ongoing quality assessment and assurance group to review quality deficiencies and develop corrective plans of action. F 2023-08-16 2023-09-13
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2023-08-16 2023-09-13
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. E 2023-08-16 2023-09-13
0909 Regularly inspect all bed frames, mattresses, and bed rails (if any) for safety; and all bed rails and mattresses must attach safely to the bed frame. E 2023-08-16 2023-09-22
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2020-02-04 2020-03-17
0585 Honor the resident's right to voice grievances without discrimination or reprisal and the facility must establish a grievance policy and make prompt efforts to resolve grievances. D 2020-02-04 2020-03-17
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. E 2020-02-04 2020-03-17
0661 Ensure necessary information is communicated to the resident, and receiving health care provider at the time of a planned discharge. D 2020-02-04 2020-03-17
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2020-02-04 2020-03-17
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2020-02-04 2020-03-17
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2020-02-04 2020-03-17
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2020-02-04 2020-03-17
0801 Employ sufficient staff with the appropriate competencies and skills sets to carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service, including a qualified dietician. D 2020-02-04 2020-03-17
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2020-02-04 2020-03-17
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. D 2020-02-04 2020-03-17
0909 Regularly inspect all bed frames, mattresses, and bed rails (if any) for safety; and all bed rails and mattresses must attach safely to the bed frame. E 2020-02-04 2020-03-17

Methodology & sources
  • Nursing Home Provider Info (nh-provider-info), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,703 rows.
  • Nursing Home Penalties (nh-penalties), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 16,915 rows.
  • Nursing Home Health Deficiencies (nh-deficiencies), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 418,972 rows.
  • Nursing Home Ownership (nh-ownership), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 160,393 rows.
  • Skilled Nursing Facility Cost Report (snf-cost-report), vintage 2023, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,120 rows.
